---
title: "Getting Started with R"
description: "A first introduction to R and RStudio — installing the software, understanding the interface, and running your first commands."
author: "Claudius Gräbner-Radkowitsch"
date: 2026-06-15
date-modified: 2026-06-15
image: ../../assets/images/placeholder.png

learning-objectives:
  - "Install R and RStudio and understand the difference between them"
  - "Navigate the RStudio interface and identify its main panels"
  - "Run basic arithmetic and assignment operations in the R console"

categories:
  - R
  - BA
  - tutorial

level:
  - BA

content-type: tutorial

citation:
  type: entry
  container-title: "Methods Hub"
  title: "Getting Started with R"
  editor:
    - name: "Claudius Gräbner-Radkowitsch"
  url: https://euf-methodology-hub.netlify.app/resources/r-programming/getting-started-with-r
---

## Introduction

*Placeholder content — this page is a structural scaffold for the Methods Hub Phase 1 build. Full content will be written in a subsequent phase.*

R is a free, open-source programming language and environment for statistical computing and graphics. RStudio is the most widely used interface (IDE) for working with R. They are separate pieces of software: R does the computation, RStudio gives you a comfortable environment to write and run R code.

## Installing R and RStudio

*Content to be added.*

## The RStudio Interface

*Content to be added.*

## Your First R Commands

*Content to be added.*

## Next Steps

Once you are comfortable with the basics, explore the [R Programming track](../../tracks/getting-started-with-r.qmd) for a suggested learning path.
